datasets: Credit Card Fraud Detection (publicly available transaction data)
Synthetic user behavior data generated using probabilistic models
description: DBSCAN-Anomaly-Detection is a density-based clustering model used for identifying anomalies in user interactions. It applies DBSCAN (Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise) to detect fraudulent transactions and unusual behavioral patterns in financial datasets.
This model clusters normal behaviors while marking low-density points as anomalies, making it effective for fraud detection and cybersecurity applications.

evaluation_metrics.silhouette_score: Measures the quality of clustering and separation.
evaluation_metrics.precision_recall_f1: Evaluates the effectiveness of anomaly detection based on precision, recall, and F1-score.
evaluation_metrics.zero_shot_performance: Tested on 10 real-world fraud detection datasets, outperforming standard k-means clustering in anomaly detection accuracy.

trained_on: A mix of publicly available credit card fraud detection datasets and synthetic behavioral anomaly datasets.
pretrained_model_source: Custom implementation available for adaptation to different fraud detection scenarios.
